description: Evaluates authentication, session, and credential management maturity—MFA, SSO, federation, risk-based auth, credential vaults, non-human identity, and SOC logging.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: Authentication, session, and credential management services exist and are reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise authentication, session, and credential services exist
Regular service efficacy review

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Authentication, session, and credential KPIs and KRIs—including MFA coverage, SSO adoption, and credential hygiene—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented KPIs and KRIs
Regular KPI/KRI review
MFA and phishing-resistant authentication coverage metrics
Failed authentication and credential attack metrics
SSO and federation coverage metrics
Session timeout and idle disconnect compliance
Credential rotation and expiry compliance metrics
Secrets and API key sprawl metrics
Passkey and passwordless credential adoption metrics

capabilities.name: Authentication system
capabilities.description: A formal authentication system authenticates subjects to network and applications enterprise-wide, supports cloud identity providers and federation, is reviewed regularly, and integrates with access control decision and enforcement points.
evaluation_focus: Formal authentication system for network and applications
Enterprise-wide authentication consistency
Cloud identity provider and federation support
Authentication service configuration review
Integration with access control decision and enforcement points

capabilities.name: Service ownership and custodianship
capabilities.description: Authentication and credential owners configure policies with central management when capabilities are siloed.
evaluation_focus: IAM engineer or authentication owner for policy configuration
Central vs location-specific authentication engineering
Dedicated credential management custodian
Separation of duties for user, authentication, and credential roles

capabilities.name: Modern authentication and credential policy
capabilities.description: Modern MFA, passwordless, and credential lifecycle policies are documented and enforced enterprise-wide with quarterly review.
evaluation_focus: Formal MFA utilization
Enterprise-wide MFA for all access
Documented passwordless authentication methodologies
Enterprise-wide vs scoped passwordless deployment
Documented phishing-resistant MFA
Enterprise-wide vs scoped phishing-resistant MFA deployment
Regular MFA and passwordless configuration review
Documented password and passkey policy for applications, networks, and endpoints
Formalized certificate age policy
Documented credential storage policy
Enterprise-wide vs vertical password policy scope
Regular password and credential policy review

capabilities.name: Risk-based authentication and session validation
capabilities.description: Risk-based authentication and continuous session validation enforce re-authentication and renewal with quarterly configuration review.
evaluation_focus: Risk-based authentication policy exists
Adaptive step-up authentication for high-risk sessions
Integration of device and behavioral signals into auth decisions
Continuous or periodic active session validation
Risk-based step-up re-authentication during sessions
Session revocation on device posture or context change
Token binding or session fixation protections

capabilities.name: Network and application session enforcement
capabilities.description: Session-based access for network and applications is enforced centrally with renewal automation, re-authentication, and regular configuration review.
evaluation_focus: Session-based network access enforcement
Session-based application access per user
Centralized session enforcement
Session renewal automation with re-authentication
Regular session policy configuration review

capabilities.name: Enterprise SSO and federation
capabilities.description: SSO, OIDC/SAML federation, SSE, and Kerberos are formally adopted enterprise-wide or for scoped applications and integrated with identity and enforcement technology.
evaluation_focus: Formal adoption of SSO, OIDC, SAML, SSE, or Kerberos
Enterprise-wide vs scoped SSO deployment
Integration with identity and enforcement technology

capabilities.name: Credential vault and lifecycle operations
capabilities.description: Secure credential vaults and lifecycle operations provision, rotate, and revoke credentials with quarterly review.
evaluation_focus: Identity store or vault manages and stores credentials securely
Automatic credential provisioning and deprovisioning
Credential vault configuration review
Integration with identity management and authentication systems
Formalized credential provisioning process
Formalized credential deprovisioning process
Formalized credential storage process
Enterprise-wide vs vertical lifecycle scope
Automated credential creation, rotation, and revocation
Regular credential lifecycle process review

capabilities.name: Non-human and API authentication
capabilities.description: Non-human identities and API authentication use governed credentials with quarterly review.
evaluation_focus: Governed service account and workload authentication
OAuth and API client credential authentication standards
Prohibition of shared credentials for non-human identities
Governed service account and API credential lifecycle
Secrets vault storage for non-human credentials
Prohibition of shared credentials for workloads and automation
Automated rotation of API keys and client secrets

capabilities.name: Authentication and session logging
capabilities.description: Authentication and session events are logged to the SOC with anomaly detection reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented authentication logging to SOC
Secure authentication event logging to SOC
Dynamic authentication changes to SOC
Anomalous authentication pattern detection
Documented session lifecycle logging to SOC
Secure session event logging to SOC
Anomalous session pattern detection

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
